title: glycosmis subopposita
intro: Glycosmis subopposita, also known as the Malay olive or bitter orange tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae, commonly found in tropical regions of Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Glycosmis subopposita include its potential to aid in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems, as well as its ability to boost the immune system. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as an herbal remedy in the form of decoctions, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
